title: arctostaphylos mewukka subsp. truei
intro: Arctostaphylos mewukka subsp. truei, also known as Mountain Manzanita, is a shrub endemic to California in the United States, characterized by its small white flowers and reddish-brown bark. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the Mountain Manzanita has been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Mountain Manzanita can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
